Defendants Peter Thompson, Toni Thompson And Thompson Pacific Constructions Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce Settlement (Ccp 664.6) To Declare June 9, 2015 Order Void Or, In The Alternative, To Reconsider That Order
Off Calendar Aug-19-2015 Advanced
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. TIMOTHY C. TRAVELSTEAD OF LEONIDOU & ROSIN, TEL. NO. 1-650-691-2888 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. ALEXANDER BANNON OF THE OLES MORRISON RINKER & BAKER LLP, TEL. NO. 1-510-903-2001 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE.

RULING - A R G U E D; Defendants Peter Thompson, Toni Thompson And Thompson Pacific Construction's Motion To Enforce Settlement (CCP 664.6) To Declare June 9, 2015 Order Void Or, In The Alternative, To Reconsider That Order is GRANTED in part. The 2010 settlement agreement and the two separate stipulations for entry of judgment, although somewhat ambiguous, do appear to contemplate two separate judgments, one to be entered against TPCI at any time, and one to be entered against the individual Thompsons in the event they defaulted in the additional $70k they agreed to pay under the settlement. It does not appear that the parties intended the individual Thompsons' $70k payment to diminish or discharge the amount of the judgment against TPCI. Plaintiffs agree, however, that prejudgment interest is not recoverable per the settlement agreement. Accordingly, the Court will modify the judgment to reduce the amount owed to $326,691 plus 10% interest from the date of entry. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order and to comply with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter : Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489 =302/eg
